title: Still Standing: How European Firms Weathered the Crisis
title_hu: Talpon maradva — hogyan vészelték át az európai vállalatok a válságot
summary_en: European firms weathered the 2008-2009 crisis better than the macro data alone would suggest. Using the EFIGE survey of around 14,000 firms across seven EU countries, the report documents how exporters and multi-product firms were systematically more resilient. The Hungarian results echo the European pattern: firms that had built international links pre-crisis lost less of their workforce and recovered faster.
policy_relevance: Direct input for Hungarian export-promotion (HIPA) and SME-finance policy after 2008. The report argues that firm-level internationalisation is itself a stabiliser, so the policy levers worth using during a downturn are export-credit access, trade finance, and FDI promotion — not blanket bailouts. Caveat: a single cross-section, so the resilience pattern may not generalise to demand-driven crises (e.g. COVID-19).
